title: Gemma Playground: Parallel Agents in Action
summary: Google DeepMind engineer Ian Ballantyne demonstrates Gemma 4 26B running locally with 10 parallel subagents coordinated by an orchestrator. The system processes concurrent tasks autonomously at over 170 tokens per second, generating an SVG art gallery coded simultaneously across agents on a single machine.
writeup: Google DeepMind engineer Ian Ballantyne runs Gemma 4 26B locally, using an orchestrator to coordinate 10 parallel subagents that process concurrent tasks autonomously. The demo hits over 170 tokens per second on a single machine and produces an SVG art gallery coded simultaneously across the agents. It illustrates a coordinator pattern for multi-agent systems paired with local inference, showing that batched, parallel agent workloads can run at the edge without a cloud backend.
